category: multi-variant page where the default variant is a small sampler

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. The default selected variant is the 50g sampler at $9.00 USD, supporting the listed price and package size fields.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as a real specialty coffee roaster product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style html-to-text renders: header/navigation, product title, price, size variants, quantity/add-to-cart, availability, tasting notes, origin details, descriptive marketing copy, shipping note, related products, embedded variant block, and footer. The voice is natural for specialty coffee—specific but not overwrought—and the copy about sampler size, roast style, and recommended resting window reads like genuine roaster language. Origin details are coherent and internally consistent: producer names, estate, region, washed process, Gesha variety, altitude, and harvest window all fit a high-end Colombian release. Pricing also feels believable for Gesha across 50g/250g/1kg tiers. The variant data block is especially realistic, with stable IDs, SKUs, names, package grams, and prices that align with the visible options. Minor tell: the exact harvest window and the polished prose could be slightly synthetic, but nothing stands out as implausible.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Elias & Shady Bayter, El Vergel Estate
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: jasmine, white peach, bergamot, cane sugar

This washed Gesha opens with a perfumed jasmine aroma, then moves into white peach and bergamot acidity with a clear cane-sugar finish.

We roast this lot light to preserve its florals and transparent citrus sweetness.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine, white peach, bergamot, cane sugar
label.coffee.producer_text: El Vergel sits on the slopes outside Fresno in Tolima, where the Bayter family has been refining careful picking and long, clean drying protocols.

coffee.producer_or_farm: Elias & Shady Bayter, El Vergel Estate
coffee.sensory_text: This washed Gesha opens with a perfumed jasmine aroma, then moves into white peach and bergamot acidity with a clear cane-sugar finish.
coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine, white peach, bergamot, cane sugar
coffee.producer_text: El Vergel sits on the slopes outside Fresno in Tolima, where the Bayter family has been refining careful picking and long, clean drying protocols. We roast this lot light to preserve its florals and transparent citrus sweetness. Best brewed as filter coffee after 7-21 days off roast.
